fact: March 15 sits exactly in the middle of March, making it the perfect balance point between winter's end and spring's beginning!
In the Northern Hemisphere, daylight and darkness are almost perfectly equal around this time - you get about 12 hours of each!
Maple trees in cold regions start their sap flowing like crazy right around March 15, which is why maple syrup season peaks now!
March 15 falls under the zodiac sign Pisces, ruled by Neptune, the planet of dreams and oceans.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, March 15 marks the heart of autumn when leaves turn brilliant orange and red!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this date - it's like nature's perfect compass!
Hibernating animals like bears and groundhogs are starting to wake up and get seriously hungry around March 15 in northern regions.
March 15 is only about 5 days away from the spring equinox, making it one of the most balanced days of the entire year for sunlight!
In many places, the soil temperature hits that magic 40-degree mark around this time, which makes earthworms go absolutely bonkers and start moving around like crazy.
When there's a full moon on March 15, it's called the 'Worm Moon' because that's when worms start popping up everywhere!
March 15 is the 74th day of the year - that's almost 20% of the entire year already gone!
In northern climates, ice on lakes and ponds gets super dangerous around March 15 because it starts getting soft and slushy from underneath.
